themes.topic: Meta's 'Iris' AI chip production and compute expansion
themes.summary: Meta plans to produce its 'Iris' AI chip from September, aiming to double compute to 14GW next year, with Broadcom as chief design partner and TSMC manufacturing. Meta has also secured long-term supply agreements with Samsung (memory), SanDisk (flash storage), and Sumitomo (fiber optic).

mentions.text:
Meta will have an AI chip codenamed ‘Iris’ in production from September as part of a plan to double compute to 14GW next year, after it deploys 7GW this year, Reuters reports:
-Iris is part of a 4-generation MTIA project from its inhouse design team.

themes.topic: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft
themes.summary: Apple has reportedly sued OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to upcoming Apple products, accusing OpenAI of encouraging employees to share confidential information for its own device development.

themes.topic: Nvidia Rubin V PCB bottleneck and Victory Giant issues
themes.summary: Nvidia's Rubin V is facing a PCB bottleneck due to Victory Giant's HDI PCB issues, specifically an impedance mismatch degrading signal integrity, which could delay resolution until late July or later.

mentions.text:
Nvidia will come out and say "no delays" for Rubin, and they will technically be correct, as TSMC won't have any problems starting chip production in September (HBM4 started shipping in June)

But the problem is with the PCB, not the chip

“Nvidia removed Kyber from its roadmap back in early May.”

_

themes.summary: An analysis argues that AI is not a bubble, contrasting current tech investment and profitability trends with the dot-com era's diverging investment and eroding profit shares, while also addressing valuation, market concentration, and circular financing concerns.

mentions.text:
AI is not a bubble.

Lazy AI bears point to $NVDA's market cap and through PTSD, claim it resembles $CSCO in March 2000.

However, any useful bearish analysis should look at what actually made the 1990s market a bubble from a macro sense, and whether those conditions exist today.

themes.topic: Apple's accelerated M-series chip roadmap for AI
themes.summary: Apple is expected to launch a base M6 this fall, skip Pro/Max/Ultra, and move directly to M7 in H1 2027, accelerating due to significant Neural Engine AI upgrades, with M7 Ultra supporting up to 1.5TB memory and M8 chips planned for 2028 on 1.4nm.

themes.topic: TSMC vs. Samsung in CPO and advanced packaging
themes.summary: TSMC is noted as leading in switch CPO, while Samsung is advancing its 2.xD packaging, which integrates HBM, logic, and silicon photonics on an interposer, aiming for a design win binding these components within twelve months.

mentions.text:

themes.summary: TSMC plans to hike prices on mature-process chip manufacturing by a single-digit percent in Q4, effective January, driven by AI boom demand for chips like PMICs.

mentions.text: TSMC plans to hike prices on mature-process chip manufacturing by a single-digit percent in the 4th quarter, media report, citing multiple unnamed chip design houses, with new pricing to take effect in January. Price hikes will vary by customer. Media surmise the AI boom is drivi

themes.topic: OpenAI's planned consumer AI device launch
themes.summary: OpenAI's first consumer AI device, potentially a smart speaker, smart glasses, or wearable pin, could launch as early as February, though its timeline might be disrupted by the ongoing dispute with Apple.

mentions.text:
The Information: OpenAI’s first consumer AI device could launch as early as February, but the Apple fight could disrupt that timeline.

The products OpenAI has reportedly explored include a display-less smart speaker, smart glasses, a digital voice recorder, and a wearable pin.

themes.topic: Nanya Technology's 2027 capex for new DRAM fab
themes.summary: Nanya Technology plans to spend NT$200 billion (US$6.2 billion) on 2027 capex, aiming for 30,000 wpm capacity by 2028 at its new fab, part of a NT$480B project.

mentions.text: Nanya Technology plans to spend NT$200 billion (US$6.2 billion) on 2027 capex, up from this year’s NT$50B+, media report. Nanya is aiming for 30,000 wafers per month (wpm) capacity by 2028 at its new fab, which will ultimately reach 45Kwpm. Around NT$100B has already been spent o

themes.topic: Samsung accelerates Yongin semiconductor cluster fab operations
themes.summary: Samsung has accelerated the start of operations for the first fab in its Yongin semiconductor cluster by two years, now targeting October 2029 for the first of six planned fabs.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: IBK Research report on Boston Dynamics humanoid robotics value chain
themes.summary: An IBK Research report maps suppliers for Boston Dynamics' Atlas robot (Hwashin, LG Energy, Hyundai Autoever, Mobis) and models humanoid volume ramps, highlighting actuator capacity as a key indicator for 2028-2030 growth.

themes.topic: Low-DK Gen 2 PCB demand surge and resilient pricing
themes.summary: Demand for Low-DK Gen 2 PCBs is surging starting 2H26 due to new GPU and ASIC chips, leading to incredibly resilient pricing and potential further increases despite supply chain expansion challenges and tough yield issues.

mentions.text: Starting 2H26, as new GPU and ASIC chips enter the sampling and mass production phases, demand for Low-DK Gen 2 is going through the roof. Even though the current supply chain is expanding as fast as they can, machinery lead times and tough yield issues mean actual output is nowh

themes.topic: CPO system constraints and timeline drivers
themes.summary: CPO's volume ramp is constrained by energy, heat, and reach, with its timeline dependent on the readiness of 800V power, liquid cooling, external-laser reliability, and optical-engine qualification, highlighting the interplay of light, electricity, and heat.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: SK Hynix ADR performance and potential Samsung ADR listing
themes.summary: SK Hynix ADR ($SKYHV) is up 16% from listing, suggesting investors may have prepared funds for the US instrument, with expectations for the Korean market to rally and questions arising about a potential Samsung Electronics ADR listing.

mentions.text:
SK Hynix ADR with ticker $SKYHV is now up 16% from listing, perhaps a lot of the softness in memory may have been investors preparing funds to move to the US instrument.

I expect that the Korean market will rip on Monday, very happy with my DRAM calls and Samsung preferreds.
